info.name: OnChainRisk Paid API
info.description:
Full OnChainRisk API for Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans.

**Setup:**
1. Sign up at https://onchainrisk.io/register and upgrade to Pro or higher
2. Dashboard → API Keys → Create a paid API key
3. Set env var `paid_api_key` to your key
4. Open any folder below — the top ones (Account → Analysis → ... → Labels) are safe to explore; the bottom ones (⚠ Advanced, ⚠ Destructive, Reference: Authentication) need extra caution.

**Scope:** mirrors https://api.onchainrisk.io/docs except for admin-only endpoints. A CI coverage script (`validate:paid-coverage`) checks no core endpoint drifts out of sync with the Swagger spec.

**Folder conventions:**
- `Account` → `Labels` — safe day-to-day paid API onboarding. All use `Authorization: Bearer {{paid_api_key}}`.
- `⚠ Advanced` — billing operations, MEV scan trigger, settings resets. Not destructive, but requires intent.
- `⚠ Destructive` — every DELETE and account deletion. Running any of these removes data.
- `Reference: Authentication` — signup / login / 2FA / password change flows. Production requires a Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA, so these are documented for schema reference; real auth is done via dashboard UI.

**Auth:** all paid endpoints accept the same API key. Auth folder endpoints that mutate auth state (change-password, 2FA, account deletion) use the browser JWT (`user_jwt` env var) — grab it from dashboard localStorage if you need to run those.

variable.description: Paid API key (Pro+). Create at https://onchainrisk.io/dashboard/api-keys
JWT from browser localStorage. Only needed for Reference: Authentication folder (change-password, 2FA) and for DELETE /api/user/account in Destructive folder.
USDC on Ethereum — safe honeypot/security test subject.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/v1/check (sync path) or POST /api/v1/multichain/analyze (async). Source: response.data.reportId.
Auto-populated when POST /api/v1/check returns 202 (heavy address routed to async queue). Source: response.data.reportId in the 202 payload.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/reports/{id}/share. Source: response.data.token.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/reports/{id}/snapshots. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/watchlist. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/labels. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated from GET /api/investigations (first item). Or run POST /api/investigate first.
Auto-populated from GET /api/keys (first returned key id).
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/support/tickets. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/billing/upgrade. Source: response.data.paymentId.

exec: pm.test('async path: returned status poll id', () => pm.expect(d.reportId).to.be.a('string'));

request.description: Public endpoint — no auth. Anyone with the share token can view the report.

item.name: Labels
item.description: Custom address labels. Bulk import/export for compliance teams.

item.name: API Keys
item.description: All /api/keys endpoints accept the paid API key (empirically verified). Sandbox-tier free users create keys via dashboard only.

request.description: Creates a new paid API key (your plan determines the max allowed). The full key is returned once in response.data.key — store it securely.

item.name: Telemetry
item.description: Lightweight client-side telemetry. Optional for external integrations.

item.name: ⚠ Advanced
item.description: Not destructive, but require intent: billing operations that affect your subscription, MEV manual scan trigger (non-free compute), settings resets, notification tests.

request.description: Triggers a manual MEV scan. Non-free compute — use sparingly; cron typically handles this automatically.

item.name: ⚠ Destructive
item.description: Every request here removes data or changes a resource irreversibly. Double-check the {{sample_*}} id variables before running — you might overwrite state you care about. Where possible, test against dummy records first.

request.description: Revokes the specified key. If you revoke the same key you're authenticating with, subsequent requests will 401.
Archives the investigation (soft removal from active list).
Requests a refund on a past payment. Refunds are processed manually after approval.
PERMANENT. Deletes the user, all API keys, all saved reports. Uses browser JWT (user_jwt) rather than API key because this operation also revokes the API keys themselves.

item.name: Reference: Authentication (CAPTCHA required)
item.description: Auth surface for schema reference only. Production register / login / forgot flows require a Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA token. The dashboard at https://onchainrisk.io handles these flows interactively; direct Postman calls typically fail CAPTCHA validation unless you extract a valid token from a browser session. Kept here so integrators can inspect the expected request/response shapes for their own auth UI.
